perturbation.original_text: a) To hold in confidence any and a ll technical or b usiness informa tion about the company’s product which is disclosed, or ma de available to you directly o r indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident t o your participation in this di scussion;
perturbation.changed_text: a) To hold in confidence any and a ll technical or b usiness informa tion about the company’s product which is disclosed, or ma de available to you directly o r indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident t o your participation in this di scussion, unless such information becomes publicly available through no fault of your own;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation. Previously, the participant was required to hold *all* disclosed information in confidence. Now, an exception exists if the information becomes public through no fault of their own. This contradicts the broader requirement of absolute confidentiality stated later in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: d) That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information beyond those ac tivities that are part of the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires holding *all* disclosed information in confidence. The changed text adds an exception where confidentiality is not required if the information becomes publicly available through no fault of their own. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the participant 'shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose to any third party' the confidential information. The change introduces an exception, while the contradicted text requires absolute confidentiality, leading to a strong in-text contradiction because the clauses cannot both be true.

perturbation.original_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit.
perturbation.changed_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit, except for ideas directly related to your pre-existing inventions.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the 'except for ideas directly related to your pre-existing inventions' clause, a contradiction is created. The original text makes all ideas the property of the company, while the added clause carves out an exception. This introduces uncertainty, conflicting with the original clear statement that *any* idea shall belong to the company. It contradicts the section below as well since it implies all ideas doesn't mean all ideas.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit, except for ideas directly related to your pre-existing inventions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except for ideas directly related to your pre-existing inventions') to the original statement that 'any ideas... shall be the property of the company'. This exception directly contradicts the original all-encompassing claim, creating a clear conflict and uncertainty about ownership of ideas conceived during the focus group. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting the in-text contradiction by including both the original all encompassing idea and the exception in the same text. Thus, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: c) The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made to you.
perturbation.changed_text: c) The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not currently available for sale; however, this does not preclude future offers for sale directly to focus group participants.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts 'no offer for sale is being made.' The modification directly contradicts this by implying that future offers for sale *may* be made specifically to focus group participants, creating an exception and thus a contradiction. This affects the section in which it exists, as it makes the offer of sale an area of ambiguity and potentially enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not currently available for sale; however, this does not preclude future offers for sale directly to focus group participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'no offer for sale is being made'. The changed text introduces an exception, stating that future offers for sale *may* be made to focus group participants. This directly contradicts the initial assertion of 'no offer for sale', creating an ambiguity regarding the possibility of future sales, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation and creating an in-text contradiction in the section it affects.

perturbation.original_text: e) All notes, reference materials, m emoranda, documentation and re cords in any way incorporating or reflecting any of the Confidential Information shall belong exclusively to Owner and the undersig ned agrees to turn ove r all copies of suc h materials in the undersigned’s possession t o Owner upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: e) All notes, reference materials, m emoranda, documentation and re cords in any way incorporating or reflecting any of the Confidential Information created solely by the company shall belong exclusively to Owner and the undersig ned agrees to turn ove r all copies of suc h materials in the undersigned’s possession t o Owner upon request.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the definition of what belongs to the Owner by adding 'created solely by the company'. The original version stated that *all* materials reflecting Confidential Information belong to the owner. Now, only materials solely created by the company are included, excluding notes taken by the focus group member themselves reflecting the same information. This is in direct contradiction to the initial paragraph stating all discussion represents Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) To hold in confidence any and a ll technical or b usiness informa tion about the company’s product which is disclosed, or ma de available to you directly o r indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident t o your participation in this di scussion, unless such information becomes publicly available through no fault of your own;
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the scope of 'Confidential Information' that belongs to the Owner by adding the qualification 'created solely by the company.' This contradicts the original scope in the contradicted text, which states that *any and all* business information disclosed or received incident to participation in the discussion should be held in confidence. The change creates a scenario where notes taken by a participant reflecting Confidential Information would no longer be considered the property of the Owner, leading to a direct contradiction and impacting enforcement of the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.original_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”), excluding their name and general demographic data. PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the definition of PII by creating an exclusion for 'name and general demographic data.' Originally, *any* PII was confidential. This modification now permits disclosure of specific types of PII, contradicting the clause that states 'I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: f) Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”), excluding their name and general demographic data. PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of PII to exclude 'name and general demographic data'. The original text and contradicted text state that *any* participant PII is confidential. Because the changed text permits disclosure of data previously considered confidential, this creates a direct conflict with the stated obligation to keep *all* PII confidential, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.changed_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s), unless explicitly stated otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'unless explicitly stated otherwise' creates a contradiction. The original text firmly establishes that all information and opinions are solely the participant's. The addition introduces a scenario where the participant can provide information/opinions reflective of their employer, conflicting with the previous statement and the purpose of an NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s), unless explicitly stated otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless explicitly stated otherwise') to the original requirement that all opinions are solely the participant's and not reflective of their employer. This directly contradicts the initial statement and introduces ambiguity about the origin and nature of the opinions provided. This creates a clear contradiction and uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the clause and the participant's obligations.
